title: In-toilet urinalysis system with capillary dispenser
abstract: We disclose an in-toilet urinalysis system which includes a system for collecting urine and applying the urine to a urine test strip. The system may include an orifice within a toilet bowl which leads into a channel. The channel may be connected to a capillary which has an orifice at each end. Urine may pass through the orifice, into the channel, then may be moved by capillary action through the capillary. A urine test strip dispenser may move reaction pads on a urine test strip to the distal end of the capillary to load the reaction pads with urine. The reaction pads may absorb the urine from the most distal orifice of the capillary. A measurable chemical reaction may occur in the reaction pads in response to urine analytes.
claims:
Claims CLAIMS  We claim: 1 . An in-toilet urinalysis system comprising: a toilet bowl, the toilet bowl comprising: an inner wall; a standing water level, and; an orifice, wherein the orifice is within the toilet bowl and above the standing water level; a captive volume within or adjacent to the toilet bowl, wherein the captive volume is sealed from toilet water entry, and wherein the following are housed within the captive volume: a channel, the channel comprising a first end and a second end, wherein a first end of the channel defines the orifice, and wherein the channel extends into the captive volume; a capillary, wherein the capillary comprises a proximal opening and a distal opening, wherein the proximal opening is in fluid connection with the second end of the channel; and a urine test strip dispenser, wherein the urine test strip dispenser positions a reaction pad of a urine test strip adjacent to the distal opening of the capillary such that the reaction pad wicks the urine from the distal opening of the capillary into the reaction pad. 2. The in-toilet urinalysis system of claim 1 , further comprising a reservoir for temporarily impeding urine flow, wherein the reservoir stands above the standing water level, wherein the orifice is within the reservoir, and wherein an underside of the reservoir and the inner wall of the toilet bowl define the captive volume. 3. The in-toilet urinalysis system of claim 2, wherein the reservoir is elongated and tilted at an angle of equal or greater than approximately 17 degrees. 4. The in-toilet urinalysis system of claim 3, wherein the reservoir comprises a first end and a second end, wherein the first end comprises a first width, and wherein the second end comprises a second width, wherein the first width is at least double the second width, and wherein the first end is higher than the second end. 5. The in-toilet urinalysis system of claim 3, in which the reservoir further comprises a removable barrier to urine flow across the reservoir. 6. The in-toilet urinalysis system of claim 5, wherein the removeable barrier comprises a gate, wherein the gate is positioned across the second end. 7. The in toilet-urinalysis system of claim 6, wherein the gate comprises at least one hinge, and wherein the at least one hinge is actuate causing the gate to open in response to a force applied by a volume of urine on the gate. 8. The in-toilet urinalysis system of claim 4, further comprising a slit-shaped opening wherein the slit-shaped opening collects a urine sample, and wherein the slit-shaped opening is in fluid connection with a device which performs a chemical analysis of the urine sample. 9. The in-toilet urinalysis system of claim 8, wherein the slit shaped opening is approximately perpendicular to the second width of the reservoir. 10. The in-toilet urinalysis system of claim 1 , wherein the channel comprises a compliant material. 1 1. The in-toilet urinalysis system of claim 1 , further comprising a valve, wherein the valve, when actuated, prevents fluid flow through the channel. 12. The in-toilet urinalysis system of claim 7, wherein the valve is selected from the following: a pinch valve and a solenoid valve. 13. The in-toilet urinalysis system of claim 7, further comprising a temperature sensor, wherein the temperature is in thermal connection with the channel, the capillary, or both the channel and the capillary. 14. The in-toilet urinalysis system of claim 9, wherein the temperature sensor is in electronic communication with a controller, and wherein the controller identifies the presence of urine in the channel or capillary based on a minimum temperature. 15. The in-toilet urinalysis system of claim 7, further comprising: at least one optical window, wherein the at least one optical window is positioned in either the channel or the capillary; and an optical sensor, wherein the optical sensor analyzes the urine sample through the at least one optical window. 16. The in-toilet urinalysis system of claim 1 1 , wherein the optical sensor is in electronic connection with a controller, wherein the controller determines the presence of urine in the channel or capillary based on optical density. 17. The in-toilet urinalysis system of claim 1 , wherein the urine test strip dispenser comprises a track for housing a urine test strip, at least two grip rollers, and a 

 motor, wherein the motor is connected to the at least two grip rollers, wherein the motor rotates the at least two grip rollers, wherein the track is positioned between the at least two grip rollers, and wherein the track moves past the distal opening of the capillary as the at least two grip rollers rotate. 18. The in-toilet urinalysis system of claim 1 , wherein the urine test strip dispenser comprises a track for housing a urine test strip and a solenoid, wherein the solenoid pushes the track past the distal opening of the capillary. 19. The in-toilet urinalysis system of claim 17, further comprising a cartridge for housing a urine test strip, wherein the cartridge feeds the urine test strip into the track. 20. The in-toilet urinalysis system of claim 18, further comprising a cartridge for housing a urine test strip, wherein the cartridge feeds the urine test strip into the track.
